Ntc Of Mtn To Amend The Complaint
Off Calendar May-18-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF PATRICK CONNALLY, DISABILITY RIGHTS ENFORCEMENT, EDUCATION, SERVICES Motion To Amend The Complaint IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery
Off Calendar May-11-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jennifer Steneberg, Esq., appearing for plaintiff/moving party: Patrick Connally
Kerin Hughey, Esq., appearing for Defendant/opposing party: FEL Properties

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Patrick Connally's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted, To Compel Appearance At Deposition is granted. Defendants are ordered to respond to Plaintiffs' Form and Special Interrogatories, Sets One and Two; and Plaintiffs' Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One and Two, within 15 days of notice of this order. Defendants are ordered to appear for deposition on a date to be noticed by Plaintiffs no sooner than May 31, 2007 and no later than June 8, 2007. Plaintiffs' Requests For Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendants' failure to respond and to appear for deposition were misuses of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions are awarded against Defendants in the amount of $1,208 to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
